title: SDUI Screen Schema
description: Server-Driven UI schema for NBA Game Detail screens

AccessibilityProperties.description: Server-provided accessibility metadata applied natively per platform

label.description: Human-readable label announced by screen readers. Omit for elements whose text content is self-describing.

role.description: Semantic role override. 'none' suppresses the element's intrinsic role.

hidden.description: When true, element and its descendants are hidden from the accessibility tree (decorative content).

headingLevel.description: Heading level (1-6) for role=heading elements. Maps to aria-level (Web), accessibilityAddTraits .isHeader (iOS), semantics { heading() } (Android).

liveRegion.description: Announces content changes. 'polite' waits for idle; 'assertive' interrupts. Maps to aria-live (Web), accessibilityLiveRegion (Android), .accessibilityAddTraits (iOS).

sortOrder.description: Override default accessibility traversal order. Lower values are visited first. Omit to use natural DOM/view order.

hint.description: Additional context announced after the label. Maps to accessibilityHint (iOS), contentDescription suffix (Android), aria-describedby text (Web).

TextVariant.description: Material3 typography scale plus legacy semantic variants and the NBA-specific `score` variant.

TextWeight.description: Font weight tokens for atomic Text elements.

visibility.description: Fraction of section area that must be visible (0.5 = 50%)

dwellMs.description: Milliseconds section must remain visible before impression fires

ImpressionPolicy.description: Impression tracking policy for analytics actions with onVisible trigger

FailurePolicy.description: Sequence behavior when an action fails. Clients apply per-type defaults when absent: navigate=halt, fireAndForget/dismiss/toast=silent, mutate/refresh=continue.

FailureFeedbackStyle.description: Presentation hint for failure feedback. Clients map to closest platform-native mechanism.

FailureFeedback.description: Optional server-provided error message and presentation style for action failures. Client falls back to generic localized string when absent.

message.description: Localized error message to display on failure

style.description: Presentation hint — clients map to closest platform-native mechanism

targetUri.description: For navigate actions: native deeplink URI

webUrl.description: For navigate actions: web-equivalent URL (first-class, not a fallback)

presentation.description: For navigate actions: how the destination is presented

modalHeight.description: For navigate actions with modal presentation: sheet height

destinations.description: For fireAndForget actions: where to send the beacon

impression.description: For fireAndForget actions with onVisible trigger: impression tracking policy

target.description: For mutate actions: state key to update. For dismiss actions: what to dismiss (modal/overlay/screen). For refresh actions: section ID to refresh (omit for full screen)

operation.description: For mutate actions: operation to perform on the state key

value.description: For mutate actions: the value to apply with the operation

endpoint.description: For refresh actions: target URL (defaults to current screen endpoint if omitted)

paramBindings.description: For refresh actions: map of query param name to screen state key, resolved at action time

message.description: For toast actions: text message to display in the toast

onFailure.description: Sequence behavior when this action fails. Client applies per-type default when absent (navigate=halt, fireAndForget/dismiss/toast=silent, mutate/refresh=continue)

failureFeedback.description: Optional server-provided error message and presentation style. Client falls back to generic localized string when absent

intervalMs.description: For poll type: interval in milliseconds

url.description: For poll/sse type: URL to poll or connect to. If omitted, polls the SDUI endpoint.

channel.description: For sse type: Ably channel name pattern (e.g., '{gameId}:linescore')

dataPath.description: JSONPath to extract section data from response (e.g., '$.game' or '$.sections[0].data')

pauseWhenOffScreen.description: Whether the client should pause this section's refresh when it scrolls out of the viewport. Default true. Set false for critical live sections (e.g., GamePanel scores) that should refresh continuously.

stringKeys.description: Optional map of targetPath to translation key for client-side i18n resolution on bound fields

BackgroundGradient.description: Gradient background with ordered color stops

colors.description: Ordered list of color stops (hex or semantic token)

BackgroundImage.description: Image background with optional scale and overlay

overlay.description: Optional overlay applied on top of the image

Background.description: Shared background type — solid color, gradient, or image with overlay

Shadow.description: Drop shadow with CSS/SwiftUI semantics (radius + offset). Compose approximates via elevation.

Badge.description: Z-positioned child element overlaid on a parent (e.g. 'LIVE' pill at bottom-right of a thumbnail, duration label). Named Badge (not Overlay) to avoid collision with the screen-level overlays map.

element.description: The element to render as a badge

alignment.description: Position of the badge within the parent bounds

AtomicElement.description: Atomic UI primitive — server-composed building block for the atomic rendering layer

accessibility.description: Server-provided accessibility metadata for this atomic element

alt.description: Deprecated: use accessibility.label instead. Retained for backward compatibility; clients prefer accessibility.label when present.

columns.description: DisplayGrid column definitions — display-only, non-interactive, server-ordered

rows.description: DisplayGrid row data — each object maps column keys to pre-formatted display values

section.description: Full section object to render via SectionRouter. Only used when type is SectionSlot.

flex.description: Flex grow factor. When set on a child of a Container, the child claims proportional space along the main axis (like CSS flex or Compose weight). Default 0 (size to content).

breakpoint.description: Responsive breakpoint in dp/px. For Container: below this screen width, direction flips from row to column. Enables responsive layouts without client logic.

cornerRadius.description: Corner radius in dp/px. Applied to Container (with overflow clip) and Image elements.

opacity.description: Element opacity (0=transparent, 1=opaque). Enables duration badge overlays and faded states.

shadow.description: Drop shadow applied to the element. Replaces elevation with richer CSS/SwiftUI shadow semantics.

badge.description: Z-positioned child element (e.g. 'LIVE' pill, duration label) overlaid on this element.

textAlign.description: Text alignment within the element. Used for centered headings, right-aligned numeric values.

showIndicators.description: Whether to show scroll indicators on ScrollContainer. Default false for clean carousel presentation.

monospacedDigits.description: Use tabular/monospaced digit rendering to prevent layout shift on numeric text changes (scores, clocks).

AtomicCompositeData.description: Data payload for AtomicComposite sections — ui contains rendering instructions, content carries domain data

ui.description: Root node of the atomic element tree — the rendering instructions

content.description: Optional domain data (strings, URLs, flags) to populate the ui tree. Reserved for future data-binding support.

TabGroupData.description: Tabbed navigation with dynamic content sections per tab

GamePanelDisplayConfig.description: Server-driven visual configuration for GamePanel — replaces the hardcoded variant branch

cardHeight.description: Fixed card height in dp/px. Null/absent = auto-size

scoreTextStyle.description: Score typography: compact = bodyLarge+Bold, prominent = headlineMedium+ExtraBold

liveBackground.description: Background override when game is LIVE

displayConfig.description: Server-driven visual configuration — controls all layout and styling knobs

clockRunning.description: Whether the game clock is actively ticking. When true, renderers should interpolate the clock locally between SSE updates for visual continuity.

visualLabel.description: Secondary label shown above the matchup (e.g. team name, 'Recommended')

Subsection.description: Nested interaction target within a section (e.g., tappable team area inside a scoreboard)

BoxscoreColumnDefinition.description: Defines a single column in the boxscore table

key.description: Property key on each player's stats object that supplies this column's value

label.description: Column header text displayed to the user

sortable.description: Whether this column supports client-side sorting

highlighted.description: Whether this column should be visually emphasised (e.g., bold)

BoxscorePlayerStatistics.description: Key-value map of stat abbreviation to value for one player row

BoxscorePlayerRow.description: One player row inside a boxscore table

starter.description: Whether this player was in the starting lineup

BoxscoreTableData.description: Typed tabular data for an NBA-style boxscore (one per team)

columns.description: Ordered list of column definitions; clients render left-to-right

players.description: Player rows ordered by server (starters first, then bench)

teamTotals.description: Aggregate row shown at the bottom of the table

sortStateKey.description: Screen-state key holding the current sort column key

sortDirectionStateKey.description: Screen-state key holding the current sort direction (asc/desc)

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when no player rows are available

FormOption.description: One selectable option within a select/radio/checkbox form field

FormField.description: One input field inside a form section

fieldType.description: Input type; clients map to platform-native controls

stateKey.description: Screen-state key that holds this field's current value

options.description: For select/radio/checkbox field types: the available choices

validationPattern.description: Optional regex pattern for client-side validation

validationMessage.description: Message to show when validation fails

FormData.description: Server-driven form section with typed fields bound to screen state

submitAction.description: Action fired when the form is submitted

subsections.description: Nested interaction targets within the section

accessibility.description: Section-level accessibility metadata (landmark role, live region, heading)

stringTable.description: Section-level map of translation key to localized string. Used by DataBindingResolver to resolve stringKeys on real-time updates.

SectionLayoutHints.description: Optional layout hints for section placement. Clients apply best-effort; unknown hints are ignored.

dividerAbove.description: Render a divider line above this section

dividerBelow.description: Render a divider line below this section

priority.description: Rendering priority hint — clients may use for lazy loading or viewport priority

SectionStates.description: Server-declared loading and error presentation for a section. Clients render these states when applicable.

skeleton.description: Which loading skeleton style to use

minHeightDp.description: Minimum height to reserve during loading (prevents layout shift)

message.description: Error message to display (e.g., 'Unable to load scores')

retryAction.description: Optional action to trigger on retry tap (typically a refresh action)

hideOnError.description: If true, collapse the section entirely on error instead of showing error UI

LeadersTableData.description: Sortable, paginated table of season statistical leaders (league-wide)

subtitle.description: Secondary text, e.g. '2025-26 Regular Season – Per Game'

columns.description: Ordered column definitions; clients render left-to-right

players.description: Player rows, pre-sorted by the server

totalRows.description: Total number of rows available server-side (for pagination display)

sortColumn.description: Key of the column the table is currently sorted by

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when no player rows are available

LeadersPlayerRow.description: One ranked player row in a season leaders table

stats.description: Stat values keyed by column key (gp, min, pts, reb, ast, etc.)

SubscribeBannerData.description: Inline subscription upsell banner with headline, body copy, and CTA

SubscribeHeroData.description: Full-screen subscription upsell hero with multi-tier pricing and feature list

name.description: Tier name, e.g. 'League Pass', 'League Pass Premium'

AdSlotData.description: Ad placement primitive — carries placement semantics while delegating auction/targeting to ad-platform SDKs (see ADR-007)

adUnitPath.description: Ad unit path used by the ad SDK

sizes.description: Accepted creative sizes as [width, height] pairs

targeting.description: Key-value targeting hints passed to ad SDK

collapseOnEmpty.description: Whether to collapse the slot when no fill is returned

label.description: Disclosure label displayed above/below the ad

VideoPlayerData.description: Video player section — platform SDK integration (DRM, HLS, ad insertion). Same justification as SubscribeHero (StoreKit/Play Billing) and AdSlot (GAM).

playerType.description: Discriminator for SDK player variant. Client passes contentId to the matching SDK method.

contentId.description: Content identifier — interpreted by playerType (gameId for game, mediaId for vod, eventId for event, streamUrl for stream). Single field avoids mutually exclusive optional IDs.

capabilities.description: Platform capabilities the player should enable. Server includes only capabilities relevant to the requesting platform (via X-Platform header).

State.description: Screen-level state map for interactive patterns

parentUri.description: URI the back button should navigate to.  Clients always show a back button; this field tells them the target.  Omit for root screens (e.g. scoreboard).

actions.description: Screen-level actions (e.g. analytics beacons, lifecycle hooks)

overlays.description: Named overlay sections the client shows when a trigger condition arises. Keys are developer-defined state names (e.g. 'couchRightsWarning'). Values are server-composed sections (typically AtomicComposite). Client controls trigger timing and presentation style; server controls display content.
